open_findings.title: Root global-error UI has no path back to portfolio (unlike app/error.tsx)
open_findings.description: Segment-level error.tsx offers Try again plus a Link to /. global-error.tsx only renders a heading, raw message, and Try again with no link to / or full layout. After a root layout failure, users lack the same recovery affordance as nested route errors.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/global-error.tsx: return body has h1, p with error.message, button reset only — no Link href="/".
apps/dashboard/app/error.tsx:38-49 — Try again plus Link href="/" labeled Portfolio for comparison.
Global root error surface (any fault that triggers global-error boundary).

history.notes: Compared global-error vs segment error components.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-576): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: Product name casing is inconsistent (Penny vs penny)
open_findings.description: README and repo branding use “Penny”; root metadata title and in-app strings use lowercase “penny” (e.g. layout metadata, document.title suffixes, footer). Decide a single public-facing rule (sentence case in prose, lowercase wordmark in UI, etc.) and align metadata, titles, and marketing copy.

proof_hooks.summary: README.md title "Penny v3.0" vs dashboard metadata.

history.notes: No prior open_findings entry; branding question for default voice.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.
Decision: Penny

open_findings.title: Portfolio project card overlay buttons rely on title= only (export/remove)
open_findings.description: Export (↓) and remove (×) controls use title attributes for tooltips but omit aria-label. Screen reader users get unclear accessible names compared to visible icon-only affordances elsewhere that use aria-label (e.g. dismiss buttons on the same page).

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/page.tsx:610-630 — buttons with title="Export" and title="Remove" only.

history.notes: Compared with other buttons on page using aria-label.

open_findings.title: Nav label “Activity” vs page headline “Job queue”
open_findings.description: Sidebar nav shows “Activity” (Shell.tsx NAV_ITEMS) while the page eyebrow also says Activity but the H1 reads “Job queue”. Not wrong, but users may wonder if they are the same section; consider aligning headline with nav or adding a short subline.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/components/Shell.tsx:89-93 — nav label "Activity" for jobs view.

open_findings.title: Browser tab title only updated on home and project pages
open_findings.description: document.title is set in app/page.tsx and ProjectPageClient for portfolio and project contexts. /activity and /repairs leave the default metadata title (“penny”), so multi-tab users cannot distinguish Activity vs Repairs from the tab alone.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/app/page.tsx:101-103 — useEffect sets document.title for portfolio only.
/activity and /repairs — no matching document.title updates found in JobQueueView or EngineView.

history.notes: grep apps/dashboard for document.title shows only two call sites.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.

open_findings.title: Orchestration enqueue secret can be read from sessionStorage by page scripts
open_findings.description: apiFetchWithEnqueueSecret merges a user-supplied enqueue secret from sessionStorage into Authorization headers. Any XSS in the dashboard origin can read sessionStorage and exfiltrate the secret.

proof_hooks.summary: Session storage value is attached as Bearer token when present.

open_findings.impact: A successful XSS can steal the shared enqueue secret and replay API calls until rotation.

suggested_fix.approach: Prefer httpOnly cookie-only auth for orchestration; if a second factor is required, use short-lived CSRF-protected forms or subresource isolation. Minimize secret lifetime in sessionStorage.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Changing auth UX may affect operators who paste secrets into the UI.

open_findings.title: Supabase helpers use select('*') with no row limits
open_findings.description: Dashboard helpers fetch entire rows for projects, audit_runs, findings, model_usage, webhooks, and schedules. Large JSON columns (metadata, proof_hooks, history) inflate payloads and memory on the server and client. getFindings has no default LIMIT, so projects with many findings pay full-table transfer per call.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace select('*') with explicit column lists for list views; add optional limit/range parameters to getFindings and callers; consider server-side pagination for the dashboard.
steps: Identify columns required by each UI surface.
Add .select('id, title, ...') and .range() or .limit() where appropriate.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-582): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: Next.js NFT tracing warns that the whole monorepo may be pulled into the server trace
open_findings.description: Production build logs report an unexpected file in the NFT list, linking next.config.ts to dynamic filesystem usage (mergeEnvLocal) and API routes importing onboarding helpers. That pattern can enlarge traced server bundles and slow cold starts on Netlify/Vercel-style deploys.

proof_hooks.value: Encountered unexpected file in NFT list — import trace: next.config.ts → lib/onboarding.ts → app/api/projects/[name]/onboarding/cluster/route.ts
mergeEnvLocal reads repo root .env.local at config load

suggested_fix.approach: Scope filesystem reads to build-time only or move env merging to runtime bootstrap; narrow onboarding imports so API routes do not drag unrelated files into the trace.
steps: Review Next 16 docs on outputFileTracingRoot and turbopackIgnore hints from the build log.
Split onboarding cluster logic so the route imports a minimal module.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-583): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: next/font Google Fonts requires network at build time
open_findings.description: layout.tsx uses next/font/google for Inter, JetBrains Mono, and DM Serif Display. The preflight build failed because Google Fonts could not be fetched (sandboxed/offline build). CI and locked-down environments need self-hosted or committed font files to avoid flaky builds and external dependency on fonts.googleapis.com during `next build`.

proof_hooks.value: Failed to fetch Inter / JetBrains Mono / DM Serif Display from Google Fonts

suggested_fix.approach: Switch to next/font/local with files in apps/dashboard/public/fonts or use a build-time font pipeline that does not require live HTTPS to Google.
steps: Vendor font files and reference via localFont.
Document network requirements for CI if keeping next/font/google.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-584): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: Portfolio audit jobs process projects sequentially
open_findings.description: After resolveProjectsForJob, the worker loops `for (const project of projects)` and runs executeProjectAudit and LLM work per project. Wall-clock time and queue depth scale linearly with portfolio size; cost scales with the number of projects × audits. Parallelism exists inside passes (PASS_CONCURRENCY) but not across projects in this loop.

proof_hooks.value: for (const project of projects) { ... await executeProjectAudit(project, payload, pool);

suggested_fix.approach: If product allows, enqueue one BullMQ child job per project or cap concurrency with a semaphore to improve throughput without exceeding provider rate limits.
steps: Define max concurrent projects per portfolio job from env.
Measure Redis/Postgres load before raising concurrency.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

open_findings.title: Project page loads the full portfolio via /api/projects
open_findings.description: ProjectPageClient uses usePortfolioProjects, which always GETs /api/projects and filters client-side to the current project. For users with many projects this duplicates work and payload size versus a single-project GET.

suggested_fix.approach: Add GET /api/projects/[name] as the primary load for the project route (or hydrate from server component) and keep list fetch for the portfolio index only.
steps: Compare existing /api/projects/[name] behavior with ProjectPageClient data needs.
Reduce redundant refetches after navigation between projects.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

open_findings.title: auditWithLlm sets maxTokens to 12288 for every audit call
open_findings.description: The worker requests up to 12k output tokens per audit call. That raises worst-case cost per call for providers that bill by generated tokens, even when findings JSON is small. Tiered limits by audit_kind or response-size heuristics would reduce tail spend.

suggested_fix.approach: Lower default maxTokens for narrow agents; keep a higher cap only for synthesis or intelligence passes; validate with token usage metrics from model_usage.
steps: Histogram completion sizes by agent from model_usage.
Set per-audit_kind limits in routing config.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

open_findings.title: Should findings have a composite index on (project_id, status, created_at)?
open_findings.description: Migrations define idx_findings_project and idx_findings_status separately. Dashboard queries often filter by project_id and optionally status with ordering by created_at. A composite index may reduce heap fetches versus BitmapAnd of two indexes, but this should be validated with real row counts and plans.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE INDEX idx_findings_project ON findings(project_id); CREATE INDEX idx_findings_status ON findings(status);
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id, title FROM findings WHERE project_id = $1 AND status = $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

suggested_fix.approach: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on production-like data; add CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on (project_id, status, created_at DESC) if seq scans or high buffer reads appear.
steps: Capture baseline plan from Supabase SQL editor.
Compare before/after index addition in a staging branch.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.
Decision: Add index

open_findings.description: pnpm run typecheck only executed @penny/shared-types and penny-dashboard (turbo output). apps/worker has no typecheck script; TypeScript is enforced via build tsc only. Risk: type errors could be missed if someone runs typecheck without build.

open_findings.title: Repository has no CI/CD pipeline
open_findings.description: There is no `.github/workflows/` directory and no automated jobs to run lint, typecheck, tests, or build on push/PR. Root `package.json` defines `lint`, `typecheck`, `test`, and `build` via Turbo, but nothing enforces them before merge or deploy.

proof_hooks.summary: Scripts exist but unrunnable in CI
proof_hooks.value: pnpm run lint | typecheck | test | build — no matching automation.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (or other hosted CI) that installs pnpm, caches store, and runs turbo lint, typecheck, test, and build on pull_request and main.
steps: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm run lint && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run test && pnpm run build

open_findings.title: Deployment target ambiguity: Netlify config coexists with Vercel project file
open_findings.description: `netlify.toml` defines the dashboard build (`turbo run build --filter penny-dashboard`) and Next plugin, while `apps/dashboard/vercel.json` specifies framework, output directory, and npm-based install/build. It is unclear which platform is authoritative for production, which affects env var naming, build commands, and review of deployment safety.

suggested_fix.approach: Document the single source of truth for dashboard hosting; remove or archive the unused platform file to avoid drift.
steps: Decide Netlify vs Vercel for dashboard
Align lockfile/install commands with pnpm workspace

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from deploy id field.

open_findings.title: Turbo lint does not cover worker TypeScript
open_findings.description: `apps/worker/package.json` has no `lint` script. Turbo runs `lint` only in workspaces that define it, so `pnpm run lint` exercises the dashboard only, not `apps/worker/src`.

proof_hooks.value: scripts: start, dev, build, admin — no lint

suggested_fix.approach: Add eslint (or biome) for `apps/worker` and a `lint` script; register in turbo.json inputs if needed.

open_findings.title: vercel.json install command does not match pnpm monorepo
open_findings.description: `apps/dashboard/vercel.json` sets `installCommand` to `npm install` while the repository uses `pnpm` and commits `pnpm-lock.yaml`. A Vercel project using this file may install inconsistent dependency trees and miss workspace hoisting.

suggested_fix.approach: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` from repo root or rely on Vercel’s pnpm detection; align with packageManager in root package.json.
steps: Set installCommand to pnpm
Document monorepo root for Vercel project settings

open_findings.title: Dashboard penny_* tables have no RLS in migrations
open_findings.description: Migrations create penny_projects, penny_audit_jobs, penny_audit_runs, penny_repair_jobs, penny_maintenance_*, penny_project_manifests, penny_linear_sync, etc., without ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or policies. The auth-scoped LYRA tables (projects, findings, …) use owner_id and policies; the dashboard document store does not mirror that model. If these tables are exposed via PostgREST with default grants, tenant isolation relies entirely on application code and connection string secrecy, not database enforcement.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS penny_projects ( ... ) — no subsequent ALTER ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY in repo migrations reviewed.
projects table uses owner_id + policies in 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql; penny_* family has no matching pattern.

suggested_fix.approach: Enable RLS on each penny_* table and define policies tied to auth.uid() (or a project membership table), or REVOKE default API role access and restrict to service_role / server-only roles.
steps: Confirm PostgREST exposure and current GRANTs in the live Supabase project.
Add migrations: ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + policies, or REVOKE FROM anon/authenticated as appropriate.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-596): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: penny_finding_status enum includes value not present in TS FindingStatus
open_findings.description: PostgreSQL enum penny_finding_status includes 'assigned' (20260402163739_lyra_schema.sql). apps/dashboard/lib/types.ts FindingStatus union does not include 'assigned', so rows or API payloads using that value will not type-check in client code and may break narrowing or switches.

suggested_fix.approach: Add 'assigned' to FindingStatus or migrate DB to drop/rename the enum value to align with the canonical contract in audits/agent expectations.
steps: Regenerate or hand-sync shared types from Supabase schema.
Run a one-time UPDATE if the value is unused.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

open_findings.title: model_usage has RLS enabled but no policy definitions in migrations
open_findings.description: 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql enables ROW LEVEL SECURITY on model_usage but does not add any CREATE POLICY. Rows are therefore denied for roles subject to RLS without a bypass. Server code using SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY bypasses RLS (see apps/dashboard/lib/supabase.ts), so ingestion may work, but authenticated or anon clients cannot read model_usage without additional policies—by design or oversight.

proof_hooks.value: ALTER TABLE model_usage ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; — file ends without model_usage policies.

suggested_fix.approach: If user-scoped dashboards need cost data under JWT, add SELECT policies joining audit_runs → projects. If only service_role reads this table, document and optionally REVOKE direct API access.
steps: Decide access model for model_usage.
Add policies or document service-only access.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

open_findings.title: repair_service migration DROP POLICY names may not match earlier migration
open_findings.description: 20260402231116_repair_service.sql drops policies named users_can_view_repair_jobs, etc. The earlier 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql creates policies named "Users can access repair jobs for their projects". IF EXISTS avoids errors, but the older FOR ALL policy may remain alongside newer SELECT/INSERT policies, producing redundant or confusing policy sets until manually reconciled.

proof_hooks.summary: DROP uses different names than CREATE in 002
proof_hooks.value: DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "users_can_view_repair_jobs" vs CREATE POLICY "Users can access repair jobs for their projects"

suggested_fix.approach: In a follow-up migration, list policies on repair_jobs in production and drop obsolete ones explicitly.
steps: SELECT polname FROM pg_policy JOIN pg_class ... WHERE relname = 'repair_jobs';
DROP redundant policies; document intended final set.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

open_findings.title: Broad GRANT on repair_jobs to anon and authenticated
open_findings.description: Migration grants SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs TO anon, authenticated. RLS still applies, but widening table privileges increases blast radius if policies are misconfigured or bypassed. Confirm this matches intended Supabase API surface.

proof_hooks.value: GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs TO anon, authenticated;

suggested_fix.approach: If only Edge Functions and server should mutate repair_jobs, prefer narrower GRANTs plus strict RLS tests.
steps: Review product requirement for client-side repair job creation.
Add automated policy tests or integration tests for anon/authenticated.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.
Decision: Narrow GRANTs

open_findings.title: FKs removed from penny_audit_* and related tables for async sync
open_findings.description: 20260403110028_fix_audit_table_schemas.sql drops foreign keys from penny_audit_jobs, penny_audit_runs, penny_orchestration_events, and penny_project_snapshots to penny_projects so jobs can exist before the project row. This trades referential integrity for pipeline flexibility; orphan rows and typos in project_name are possible.

suggested_fix.approach: Periodic cleanup job or CHECK using validated project_name from application layer; optional deferred FK via materialized project registry.
